task_completion.description: Configure the docker-listener module in Wazuh
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal showing the agent editing '/var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf' (e.g., using vi, nano, or sed) to enable or add the '<wodle name="docker-listener">' module block.

task_completion.description: Create a custom detection rule for the unauthorized image
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal showing the agent adding a rule to '/var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml' with '<rule id="100205" level="12">' and a condition matching the 'crypto-miner:latest' or 'crypto-miner' image.

task_completion.description: Restart the Wazuh manager to apply configurations
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal command restarting the Wazuh manager, such as 'docker exec wazuh-wazuh.manager-1 wazuh-control restart' or restarting the Wazuh docker container itself.

task_completion.description: Trigger the alert by running the unauthorized container
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal command executing a docker container with the image 'crypto-miner:latest' (e.g., 'docker run crypto-miner:latest' or similar).

task_completion.description: Verify the alert was triggered in the Wazuh logs
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal showing the agent checking '/var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.json' (e.g., using cat, tail, or grep) and displaying a JSON log entry containing '"rule":{"id":"100205"' and references to the 'crypto-miner' image.

integrity.description: Verify that configuration files were genuinely edited
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent uses standard text editors (nano, vim) or valid shell commands to modify the actual Wazuh configuration files inside the container, rather than creating fake local files.

integrity.description: Verify the unauthorized container was actually executed
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the agent actually executes the 'docker run' command for the crypto-miner image and that the terminal shows the expected Docker output (e.g., pulling image or container execution), not just a typed command without execution.

integrity.description: Verify the alert log is a genuine system output
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the alert log output appears as a properly formatted JSON string typical of Wazuh alerts, and was generated by the system (e.g., via tail or grep on the log file), not manually echoed by the agent.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Wazuh container is named 'wazuh-wazuh.manager-1'. The configuration file is located at '/var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf' and the custom rules file is at '/var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml' inside the container. The target rule ID is 100205 and the target image is 'crypto-miner:latest'. The alerts log is located at '/var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.json'.
